new_classes.definition_en: The Tokaj wine production region, a protected designation of origin (PDO) area located in north-eastern Hungary and a small part of Slovakia, in which Tokaj wines must be produced.
The process of producing Tokaj wine within the Tokaj protected designation of origin region, in accordance with the conditions specified in additional note 4(B)(b) to Chapter 22 of the CN.

new_classes.label_en: Tokaj wine, in containers holding ≤ 2 l (CN 2204 21 23)
new_classes.label_de: Tokajer Wein, in Behältnissen mit einem Inhalt von 2 l oder weniger (KN 2204 21 23)
new_classes.definition_en: Wine bearing the Tokaj protected designation of origin (PDO), produced in the Tokaj region in accordance with additional note 4(B)(b) to Chapter 22, presented in containers of 2 litres or less.

coverage_explanation: The three main discriminating criteria are captured: (1) Tokaj PDO geographic origin (via TokajRegionSite + TokajWineProductionProcess + occurs_in), (2) small container ≤ 2 l (via SmallContainer existential restriction), and (3) membership in the wine-from-fresh-grapes heading 2204 (parent class). The reference to additional note 4(B)(b) to Chapter 22 specifies detailed quality and production conditions for Tokaj wine (e.g. grape varieties, minimum alcohol, residual sugar categories such as Aszú, Szamorodni, etc.) that cannot be fully encoded without introducing many additional quality subclasses for each Tokaj wine style. These sub-criteria of note 4(B)(b) are acknowledged in the coverage_explanation but are not individually modelled, resulting in a score of 0.85 rather than 1.0.
